title: catha excisa
intro: Catha excisa, also known as khat or Abyssinian tea, is a flowering plant native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula. It is traditionally consumed for its stimulating effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid content, particularly cathinone and cathine. The medicinal properties of Catha excisa include its ability to increase alertness, boost energy, and alleviate fatigue, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Catha excisa is typically prepared by drying and chewing the leaves or stems, or by brewing them as a tea, with some herbal preparations also available in capsule or powder form.
